en.extract:
The Madagascar cuckoo or Madagascan cuckoo (Cuculus rochii), also known as the Madagascar lesser cuckoo, is a species of cuckoo in the family Cuculidae.  Though it breeds only in Madagascar, it winters to eastern Africa (from the African Great Lakes region to Zanzibar).


Description
The Madagascar cuckoo is a small, slim cuckoo, measuring 28 cm (11 in) in length.

The '''Madagascar cuckoo''' or '''Madagascan cuckoo''' ('''''Cuculus rochii'''''), also known as the '''Madagascar lesser cuckoo''', is a species of [[cuckoo]] in the family [[Cuculidae]].  Though it breeds only in [[Madagascar]], it winters to eastern Africa (from the [[African Great Lakes]] region to [[Zanzibar]]).

==Description==
The Madagascar cuckoo is a small, slim cuckoo, measuring {{convert|28|cm|in|abbr=on}} in length.<ref name="Morris">{{cite book |title=Birds of Madagascar: A Photographic Guide |first1=Pete |last1=Morris |first2=Frank |last2=Hawkins |publisher=Pica Press |year=1998 |location=Mountfield, East Sussex, UK |isbn=1-873403-45-3 |page=188}}</ref>
